awards.title: Excellence Award
awards.summary: Given in recognition of outstanding delivery of our Total Cloud Data Center provisioning and network automation solution.

basics.name: Shane Ray
basics.label: Senior Software Engineer · Applied AI
basics.summary: Senior software engineer at Uber (U4B Engineering — vouchers and employee mobility) since May 2022, with an prior year on Uber Transit and Vouchers as an extended contract engineer (~5 years on the Uber stack). Backend and full-stack IC on distributed Go services, billing and trip lifecycle, admin web experiences, and CRM-backed communications. Applied AI for engineering: agent workspaces, prompt-driven codemods at scale, and investigation tooling for cross-service RCA. Earlier career: .NET technical lead, cloud automation, and CI/CD at telecom SaaS companies.

projects.name: IC agent workspace (super-shane)
projects.description: Persistent, hook-governed agent environment for vouchers on-call RCA, diff authoring, and structured memory.
highlights: Single dispatcher over registered tools; tiered pre-flight context (~55% lower bootstrap token use vs unstructured chat sessions, measured internally).
Task-runner dashboard, mail/docs/ticket ingest, conventions catalog, and deterministic investigation capture.
Presented at internal Eng AI Happy Hour (May 2026) as IC plus agent pairing for production engineering.

projects.name: AI-driven Enzyme test stack migration
projects.description: Led U4B Vouchers deprecation of 850+ legacy Enzyme test files using prompt-engineered, review-gated codemods.
highlights: Methodology adopted by Global Support Services and other teams after IC handoff.
Preserved CI, lint, and coverage gates while reducing manual migration labor.

projects.name: Go Interface Shadow Runner
projects.description: Generic Go library for live shadow traffic when changing primary datastores or query paths.
highlights: Used during vouchers Elasticsearch-to-EVA/Pinot migration (Apr 2023–Jan 2024) with built-in migration dashboards.
Designed for reuse by other teams performing datastore cutovers.

references.name: Phil Scott (Senior Architect)
references.reference: Shane is a tireless worker and learner. He not only looks to constantly improve his work, but also take what he's learned and share it with others.

references.name: Ashish Arora (Manager Software Development)
references.reference: Shane is an excellent software developer. He takes a great deal of pride in what he does and he works very hard to achieve the exact results that the customer is looking for. He has worked many late hours as part of my development team just to make certain that a delivered project is exactly what the customer wanted. Shane is passionate about writing code and solving complex problems through development of creative and innovative software. He is conceptually very strong and stays up-to-date with the latest techniques and tools related to software development.

references.name: Shannon Callaway (Cloud Technology Strategist)
references.reference: Shane was a fantastic employee. He was an extremely dedicated worker who consistently worked to improve his skill set and rise to new challenges. He was personable, dependable, and able to quickly adapt to new situations.

references.name: Reference Contact Information
references.reference: Contact information for the listed references are available upon request.

work.summary: Senior IC on U4B Vouchers and employee mobility (cost center U4B Engineering): distributed Go services, billing and trip lifecycle, web-vouchers admin app, CRM transactional messaging, and backend on-call. Remote (Louisville, KY).
highlights: Built and operate super-shane, a persistent IC agent workspace (task runner, ingest, tool dispatch); ~55% lower bootstrap context vs unstructured LLM sessions; demo at Eng AI Happy Hour (May 2026).
Led AI-assisted deprecation of 850+ Enzyme test files; approach reused by Global Support Services and other teams after handoff.
Led vouchers Elasticsearch-to-EVA/Pinot migration (Apr 2023–Jan 2024) without downtime; authored Go Interface Shadow Runner and Neutrino client libraries.
Owned SMS rich link previews (Open Graph on voucher claim pages) from spec through production (Apr 2025).
Investigated capital-bill ingestion loss and voucher tips settling to personal payment methods (H1 2026): DWH-scale datasets, billing-engine tracing, multi-team fixes in flight.
Drove Uber Me Quick Send backend and SDUI paths, geolocation autoclaim, and web-vouchers performance rehaul (50+ memoized selectors).
Cut voucher transactional email failure rate from over 17% to 0–0.5% via Bloc template fixes and observability adopted across U4B CRM.
Led ~50% on-call alert reduction initiative; documented GSS handoff saving 120+ vouchers engineer hours; Uber Technology and Velocity peer awards.

work.summary: Extended contract engineer on Uber Transit (Jun–Nov 2021) then U4B Vouchers (Dec 2021–May 2022); converted to full-time SWE II on 2022-05-16.
highlights: Transit: removed Morpheus experiment dependencies and migrated flags to Flipr ahead of Morpheus decommission.
Vouchers: guest search, cancel voucher/code APIs, email failure observability (failure rate improvement program), and admin dashboard V3 foundations.

work.summary: Responsible for the overall planning, execution and success of our internal software.
highlights: Created architecture and developed new customer account portal to replace existing aging solution
Created architecture and developed new online marketplace to allow potential customers to order services online.
Designed and built development environments and automated deployment tools.
Identified and resolved technical debt.
Migrated core libraries and services to .NET Core/.NET Standard
Mentor and provide guidance for other developers, both junior and senior.
Worked closely with business owners, operations, and other developers to ensure projects where of high quality and completed on time.
Worked with key business stakeholders during acquisitions for knowledge share and system integration.

work.summary: Member of the core services team responsible for partnering and interacting with technical and business contacts to transform, enhance, and build efficiencies.
highlights: Improved unit/integration tests performance.
Working with DevOps team to improve automated testing/release of core WCF services.
Migrated core libraries and services to .NET Core/.NET Standard
Created architecture and developed new backend payment processor to replace existing hard to manage payment processor.
Mentor and provide guidance for other developers, both junior and senior.

work.summary: Helped lead efforts of migration of company wide primary B/OSS legacy WCF client / server application to Web API and AngularJS system.
highlights: Maintained and improved primary business Portal used by both internal employees and customers utilizing C#, ASP.Net 4, HTML5, CSS3 and JavaScript / jQuery.
Used Git Flow development process via Atlassian JIRA and Stash.
Implemented an automated continuous delivery system using TeamCity, Jenkins, and Octopus Deploy.
Worked closely with Senior Architect to establish best practices for REST API resources, and documentation via Swagger.
Worked with key business stakeholders during acquisition for knowledge share and system integration.
Managed, Onboarded, and collaborated with geographically diverse teams to ensure project delivery dates were met.
Conducted development continuous education, and training on new policies and technology such as unit testing, GitFlow workflow, message queuing, and general developer productivity tips
Performed code reviews on committed line-of-business code to ensure standards and quality, security, and performance were met.
Worked with offshore development teams to find gaps in knowledge and documentation and updated internal documentation to fill those gaps.
Focused on security and performance.

work.summary: Responsible for engineering new products and services.  Leveraging current and new technologies to provide cutting edge new and exciting products for our customers.  Also responsible for refreshing current offering and products to stay in tune with today's market.
highlights: Developed and maintained many APIs and tools for fellow cloud engineers.

Designed architecture, developed, and maintained network management API.  This API provided provisioning, management, logging, and data retrieval for multiple of the following network devices: Fortinet Forigate, Juniper NetScreen, Cisco ASA, Cisco NSX, and Palo Alto.

Designed architecture, developed, and maintained VMware vSphere/VMware orchestrator management API.  This API provided provisioning, management, logging, and data retrieval for our VMware virtual machines.

work.summary: Responsible for creating and re-defining processes for support staff.  Responsible for Tier 3 Support escalations.  Continued to develop tools and re-work systems to make Tier 2 Support teams and Tier 3 support more efficient, productive, and happy.  Took on new product development working to integrate tools, processes, and added automation to provide a better customer experience.  Worked heavily with System Center, Hyper-V manager, Fortinet Fortigate 3950B and FortiOS 4.11, PowerShell, Cisco UCS, and others.
highlights: Took initiative to develop tools and re-work systems to make Tier 2 Support teams more efficient, productive, and happy.

work.summary: Tier 2 Support Engineer for cloud hosting products including but not limited to IIS, WebDAV, FTP, MSSQL, MySQL, dedicated and virtual hosted servers.
highlights: Took initiative to develop tools and re-work systems to make Tier 2 Support teams more efficient, productive, and happy.

work.summary: Answered calls for AppleCare CPU tech support. I was responsible for asking relevant probing questions and analyzing the information the customer provided.  Then I would isolate the customers issue to hardware or software and offer the best possible resolution.
highlights: I provided solutions to many networking, security, and DNS issues to maintain productivity on the call floor.  Also made some tools to help advisers through their day to increase productivity and limit distractions.  I have also identified many security holes and provided patches or fixes for them.

SykesLinks – Custom home page that puts all adviser tools in one place.  Also includes a simplified Kronos (time card).  This also gives the supervisor easy access to see which Kronos code that the advisers were in.
Apple Support Sites – The apple support websites that advisers use often suffered from a DNS issue that caused loading times to reach 5+ min.  Identified problems, found solution and provided patch.
Provided excellent customer service and often received support member of the week based on customer satisfaction surveys.
Worked with many different aspects of the Apple computer.  Such as, but not limited to: Email, Networking Issues (wired, wireless), and Hardware/Software Issues

work.summary: Developed and edited many complex websites with ASP.NET, C#, PHP, MySQL, HTML, CSS and JavaScript.
highlights: Designed Graphics to make the overall site design more appealing.
Optimized Code and graphics for faster loading.
Customer Relations, to make sure the customer gets what they need when they needed it.

work.summary: Inspected and repaired various Sanyo/Sprint cellphones down to component level.
highlights: Soldering on a daily basis
Worked as a member of the Quality Control team
